new_classes.label_en: PDO wine (other than Tokaj), actual alcoholic strength ≤ 15% vol, in containers > 2 l and ≤ 10 l — other (CN 2204 29 38)
new_classes.label_de: Wein mit g.U. (außer Tokaj), tatsächlicher Alkoholgehalt ≤ 15 % vol, in Behältnissen mit mehr als 2 l bis 10 l — anderer (KN 2204 29 38)
new_classes.definition_en: Wine of fresh grapes bearing a protected designation of origin (PDO/g.U.) other than Tokaj, with an actual alcoholic strength not exceeding 15% vol, presented in containers holding more than 2 litres and not exceeding 10 litres — other than semi-sparkling wine. Corresponds to CN subheading 2204 29 38 and falls under additional note 6(a) of Chapter 22.

coverage_explanation: The main discriminating criteria are captured: (1) PDO wine quality (not Tokaj), (2) container volume > 2 L and ≤ 10 L (MediumContainer2To10L), (3) actual alcoholic strength ≤ 15% vol, (4) exclusion of semi-sparkling wine. The legal note references additional note 6(a) of Chapter 22 defining what constitutes a PDO wine, which is handled via the existing PDOWineQuality class. A minor gap remains: the lower bound of container volume (> 2 L) is semantically encoded in MediumContainer2To10L but the ≤ 10 L upper bound is not formally axiomatised in the TBox beyond the class label — this is a pre-existing TBox limitation. Additionally, the distinction from 2204 29 28 (another PDO wine large container code with alcoholic strength > 15% vol or Tokaj) is implicit via the complement and range restrictions but not fully verified against all sibling codes in the 2204 29 range. The reference to additional note 6(a) cannot be directly linked as a named individual in the current TBox.
